steps.title: Use the logging result
steps.content: The study found that plots with limited logging may be more robust than unlogged plots. The blank must draw a cautious conclusion from that result: limited logging can support forest health, not that all logging or unrelated strategies are best.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice B brings in other strategies that were not compared. Choice C says the benefits may be unknowable, but the study already reports positive results. Choice D says untouched forests may be best, which contradicts the comparison with unlogged plots. The remaining option keeps the conclusion limited to controlled logging. The answer is A.
